description_long: This is a compact, World War II-era submachine gun with a dark metallic finish and a streamlined, utilitarian design. The rifle features a cylindrical receiver, a prominent detachable box magazine inserted vertically below the action, and a short, straight barrel with a simple front sight post. The stock is a minimalistic, integrated pistol grip style, lacking a traditional shoulder stock, which makes the weapon more compact and maneuverable. The surface is primarily matte black with subtle metallic highlights, and the construction appears robust, with visible seams and mechanical details. The overall form is functional, with few decorative elements, emphasizing practicality and ease of use in close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, dark metallic submachine gun with a vertical box magazine and a minimal pistol grip stock, designed for practical use in close-quarters.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, pistol grip, and vertical box magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel, front sight post, and the narrow profile of the receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the magazine, grip, and receiver details.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the receiver and the alignment of the barrel.
